## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 30, 1659, Dara Shikoh, the incredibly massive, brilliant intellectual, and heavily favored heir apparent to the massive Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan, was brutally and aggressively executed in Delhi. Following his incredibly massive, devastating defeat at the Battle of Samugarh by his fiercely orthodox, massive younger brother Aurangzeb, he was heavily captured and beheaded. His massive execution absolutely destroyed the massive, syncretic, and heavily tolerant religious trajectory of the massive Mughal Empire, heavily plunging it into an era of fierce religious orthodoxy.

On August 30, 1928, the massive, highly influential, and fiercely nationalist Independence for India League was officially and aggressively founded. Heavily championed by incredibly prominent leaders like Jawaharlal Nehru and Subhas Chandra Bose, the massive organization aggressively emerged as a fierce, radical pressure group within the Indian National Congress. They heavily and completely rejected the moderate demand for "Dominion Status," heavily insisting on nothing less than absolute, uncompromising, and massive total independence (Purna Swaraj) from the oppressive British Empire.
